subject: feat(icons): new stacked-modules icon driven by DaisyUI business tokens
body:
Replaces the slate-and-white S lettermark (leftover from pre-DaisyUI) with a
three-bar stack representing the product's core concept: agreements assembled
from independent text modules. Colours are DaisyUI `business` theme tokens
(accent / secondary / base-content on base-100), so the icon aesthetic aligns
with the installed-PWA chrome and any future theme tweak flows in on the next
`npm run generate-icons`.

- `public/icon-source.svg` is now a token template (`{{business.*}}`
  placeholders) — zero hex literals.
- `scripts/daisyTokens.mjs` extracts the oklch→hex helper shared by
  `generate-icons.mjs` (token substitution) and `generate-theme-meta.mjs`
  (combo metaColor verification), so one math bug can't drift between scripts.
- `generate-icons.mjs` resolves `{{themeName.token-name}}` at build time,
  fails loud on unknown tokens or malformed `{{…}}`.
- CLAUDE.md APP_ICONS section reflects the new pipeline + current design.

Tags: pwa, icons, ui, refactor
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: theme-system
Semver: minor
